text: The surgeon demonstrates a laparoscopic repair of an inguinal hernia without mesh, known as ileopubic tract repair. The hernia defect is visible, with the iliopubic ligament on the right side of the image. The surgeon sews the iliopubic ligament to the abdominal wall muscle. The peritoneum is opened, revealing the preperitoneal space, and the hernia sac and fat are removed. In this female patient, the round ligament is visible on the left, passing through the hernia defect. The defect is repaired with absorbable sutures, and the peritoneum is closed with absorbable sutures.
